title: West King Street Renewal LLP: application made to abstract water
body: "Details of the application  West King Street Renewal LLP has applied to the Environment Agency for a licence.  The Environment Agency is giving notice of this application in accordance with Section 37 of the Water Resources Act 1991 and Regulation 6 of the Water Resources (Abstraction and Impounding) Regulations 2006.  The application is for a full licence to abstract water from underground strata comprising of Chalk at Hammersmith Town Hall, King Street, London at National Grid Reference (NGR) TQ 22666 78419.  The application is to abstract water as follows:    119 cubic metres an hour  1,042 cubic metres a day  380,160 cubic metres a year   The water will be used for an Open Loop Ground Source Heat Pump System for a proposed development.  Further details are: a portion of the water will be returned to the Chalk aquifer via a re-injection borehole at NGR TQ 22639 78526.  How to view the application  Please contact us via email or use the number below to arrange to see the application documents.  How to comment on the application  Send any representation about this application by email, quoting the name of the applicant and reference number NPS/WR/040031 to the Environment Agency at: PSC-WaterResources@environment-agency.gov.uk  By 20 March 2024.  For advice about how to make a representation call 03708 506 506.  "
